---
title: With Provider
description: Add Qora to an existing Provider app to specialize server-state handling while preserving Provider for UI state and dependency injection.
navigation:
  icon: i-lucide-plug
---

Provider and `ChangeNotifier` are effective for UI state and dependency injection. Server data management introduces repetitive boilerplate in each notifier: `isLoading`, `error`, manual cache guards, and inconsistent retry handling.

Qora takes over that specific responsibility. Provider keeps everything else.

---

## The Problem with ChangeNotifier for Server Data

A typical data-fetching notifier:

```dart [lib/providers/user_provider.dart]
class UserNotifier extends ChangeNotifier {
  User? data;
  bool isLoading = false;
  Object? error;
  int? _loadedId;

  Future<void> fetchUser(int id) async {
    if (_loadedId == id && data != null) return; // manual cache, no TTL, no SWR
    isLoading = true;
    error = null;
    notifyListeners();
    try {
      data = await UserApi.getUser(id);
      _loadedId = id;
    } catch (e) {
      error = e; // no retry, no backoff
    } finally {
      isLoading = false;
      notifyListeners();
    }
  }
}
```

```dart [lib/user_screen.dart]
Consumer<UserNotifier>(
  builder: (context, notifier, _) {
    if (notifier.isLoading) return const CircularProgressIndicator();
    if (notifier.error != null) return ErrorWidget('${notifier.error}');
    if (notifier.data == null) {
      notifier.fetchUser(userId); // side effect in build, fragile lifecycle
      return const SizedBox.shrink();
    }
    return UserCard(notifier.data!);
  },
)
```

This pattern is written identically for every API call. It also misses: stale-while-revalidate, background refetch on focus/reconnect, request deduplication, and offline support.

---

## After: Let Qora Own Server Data

```dart [lib/main.dart]
final qoraClient = QoraClient(
  config: const QoraClientConfig(
    defaultOptions: QoraOptions(staleTime: Duration(minutes: 5)),
  ),
);

runApp(
  MultiProvider(
    providers: [
      ChangeNotifierProvider(create: (_) => ThemeNotifier()),
      ChangeNotifierProvider(create: (_) => AuthNotifier()),
    ],
    child: QoraScope(
      client: qoraClient,
      lifecycleManager: FlutterLifecycleManager(qoraClient: qoraClient),
      connectivityManager: FlutterConnectivityManager(),
      child: const MyApp(),
    ),
  ),
);
```

```dart [lib/user_screen.dart]
QoraBuilder<User>(
  queryKey: ['users', userId],
  fetcher: () => UserApi.getUser(userId),
  builder: (context, state, fetchStatus) => switch (state) {
    Success(:final data) => UserCard(data),
    Loading() => const CircularProgressIndicator(),
    Failure(:final error) => ErrorWidget('$error'),
    Initial() => const SizedBox.shrink(),
  },
)
```

The Qora version removes manual loading flags, notifier notifications, and custom cache guards. Retry, request deduplication, background revalidation, and SWR behavior are built in.

The third builder argument `fetchStatus` distinguishes `FetchStatus.fetching` from `FetchStatus.paused` in offline conditions. Use this state to render a waiting-for-connection indicator instead of a loading spinner.

---

## Division of Responsibilities

| Responsibility                                     | Keep in Provider                       | Move to Qora                  |
| -------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| Data fetched from an API (users, products, feeds)  |                                        | `QoraBuilder` + `fetcher:`    |
| Mutations (create, update, delete)                 |                                        | `QoraMutationBuilder`         |
| UI state (selected tab, modal open, form values)   | `ChangeNotifier`                       |                               |
| Authentication session (token in memory)           | `ChangeNotifier`                       |                               |
| Dependency injection (services, repositories)      | `Provider` / `ChangeNotifierProvider`  |                               |

---

## Key API Mapping

| Provider pattern                                      | Qora equivalent                  |
| ----------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| `ChangeNotifier` + `fetch()` method                   | `QoraBuilder` with `fetcher:`    |
| `Consumer` + ternary on `isLoading / error / data`    | `switch (state)` on `QoraState`  |
| Manual `if (data != null) return`                     | `staleTime` in `QoraOptions`     |
| `ChangeNotifierProvider` (data layer)                 | `QoraScope`                      |
| `notifyListeners()` after write                       | `client.invalidate(key)`         |

---

## Checklist

- [ ] Add `qora_flutter` to `pubspec.yaml` and run `flutter pub get`
- [ ] Nest `QoraScope` inside existing `MultiProvider` root (not a replacement)
- [ ] Identify `ChangeNotifier` classes that only fetch and emit loading/error/data and replace them with `QoraBuilder` (`fetcher:`, 3-arg builder)
- [ ] Replace `Consumer` loading/error/data ternaries with `switch (state)`
- [ ] Replace manual cache guards with `staleTime`
- [ ] Replace `notifyListeners()` post-write with `client.invalidate(key)`
- [ ] Keep `ChangeNotifierProvider` entries for UI state, auth, and DI

---
